reason: The item has strong diagnostic intent around environment–rate interaction and mechanistic sequencing, but several options hinge on broad modal language (e.g., “demonstrates,” “always,” “consistently”) that can make boundary conditions feel under-specified rather than tightly entailed. Decoys cluster around scope/monotonicity overreach, reducing single-fault purity and making elimination somewhat pattern-based instead of mechanism-based; tightening option phrasing and diversifying decoy failure types would increase discriminative separation.
